items.title: Syrian ex-rebel factions agree to merge under defense ministry | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Syria's de facto leader Ahmed al-Sharaa reached an agreement on Tuesday with former rebel faction chiefs to dissolve all groups and consolidate them under the defense ministry, according to a statement from the new administration. 
#News ## #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: American Airlines flights resume after Christmas Eve tech issue | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
American Airlines said its flights resumed after a technical glitch forced the carrier to issue an hour-long ground stop, disrupting travel for thousands on Christmas Eve, one of the busiest periods of the year.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Starbucks workers expand US strike | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
A strike at Starbucks will expand to over 300 US stores with more than 5,000 workers expected to walk off the job before the five-day work stoppage ends later on Christmas Eve, the workers' union said.

items.title: Democracy heads into 2025 bloodied but unbowed
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Over 2 billion people went to the polls in 2024, more than ever before. From India to Ireland, the UK to Uruguay, Senegal to South Africa, voters went out to do their civic duty. Mark Bendeich explains why democracy looks bruised but not beaten as it heads into 2025.

#Reuters #News #Election #NewYear #Newsfeed
